ADJ.etymology: From annar (accusative annan), from annarr, from *anþeraz, from *ánteros.
From lǣtter, from léttr, from *linhtaz, from *h₁lengʷʰ-.

ADV.etymology: Compound of t2=mind, literally "to one's mind".

NOUN.etymology: gem-pro krîch. Unlike - and krig, the Swedish word has neutrum gender like kríggj.
From vika, from vika, from *wikǭ, from to bend, wind, turn, yield.
From fall, from to fall. The grammatical sense is a calque of Latin casus.
From mānadagher, from mánadagr, from *mānini dag, a calque of day of the moon, equivalent of t2=day. Cognate with mandag, måndag, mandag, mánudagur, mánadagur, maanantai, Monday, Montag and maandag.
sǫk, from *sakō. Cognate with sak, sak, sag, sök, sake, zaak, Saak, Sook, Sache. An unrelated word that also underwent the transformation in meaning from "legal matter" to "thing, item" is causa.
gmq-osw:ār From ār, from ár, from *jērą, from *yeh₁r-.
From beslut, from beslut, a nocap=1. 1513. Compare besluit, Beschluss.
From hūs]], from hús, from house.
From persona. the late 14th century. persona.
From stormber, from stormr, from *sturmaz, from t=to rotate, swirl, twirl, move around.
From sȳn, from sýn, sjón, from *siuniz.
From væruld, værald, værild, værld, from verǫld, from *weraldiz. In (intensifier), perhaps elliptical for "världens största" (the world's largest) or the like.
From dumber, from dumbr, from *dumbaz, from *dʰewbʰ-. Compare dumb, dum, dumbur, dumb, dom and dumm.
From dygn, dyghn, døgn, døghn, from dœgn, from *dōgin-, alternative form of *dōg-. mid-14th century.SO}} Cognate with døgn, døgn and døgn. See also dœgr, døger and dægur.
From klockæ, from klokka, from clocca, probably of - origin, from bell (compare cloch, clog), from *klēg-, *klōg- onomatopoeia.
Borrowed from critique, from criticus, from κριτικός.
From linia, from linea, from , from flax.
From maþer, mander, from maðr, from *mann-.

The word is originally a short form for ''Odens dag'', meaning ''day of Oden''. (Note: "Oden" and "Odin" are alternative spellings of the same name.) From oþinsdagher, from Odin's day, from *Wōdanas dag.
From raust, ultimately from a - derivative of t=to flow, boil, stream, move.922 Related to t=river. Cognate with røst.
From spil, from spil and spil, from *spil.
From ūtganger, from útgangr, from *ūtgangaz. Equivalent to gång.
From ærande, ærinde, ærende, from erendi, ørendi, errand, from *airundiją. Cognate with ærinde, ærend, dialectal Erend and errand.

PRON.etymology: From maþer, mander, from maðr, from *mann-.
From annar (accusative annan), from annarr, from *anþeraz, from *ánteros.

VERB.etymology: From gilda, from gilda. Compare gilda and gildr.
From lēþa, from leiða, from *laidijaną. Seems like it only applies to the verb
